title: Dendrochilum trilobum
intro: Dendrochilum trilobum is a species of orchid native to Southeast Asia, commonly known as the "three-lobed Dendrochilum". This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in treating various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Dendrochilum trilobum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Dendrochilum trilobum is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of a tincture or a powder to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Dendrochilum trilobum is a species of orchid, classified within the family Orchidaceae. The genus Dendrochilum is a part of the subtribe Malaxideinae, which is a group of terrestrial orchids. This species is further categorized as a member of the tribe Malaxideae. Dendrochilum trilobum is native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia, specifically in countries such as Malaysia and Indonesia. The exact classification of Dendrochilum trilobum may vary depending on the source and the most recent taxonomic revisions.
